hero_summary: Ajam Ushayran belongs to the Ajam family. In classical Turkish theory it is understood as a transposition of Jaharkah to Ajam Ashiran (B-flat tonic below), with a basically descending sayr.
short_identity: An Ajam-family maqam resolving on Ajam Ashiran, with a major/Jaharkah character, a descending tendency, and a strong center on Ajam in the middle register.

quick_facts.karar: Ajam Ashiran (B-flat below / low B-flat tonic)
quick_facts.lower_jins: Jins Ajam on Ajam Ashiran in practical Arabic explanation
quick_facts.character: Bright Ajam/Jaharkah color with a descending sayr
quick_facts.common_start: It tends to center first in the upper or middle area, then descends to confirm Ajam Ashiran as the final tonic

sayr.summary: Ajam Ushayran is a maqam of the Ajam family. In classical Turkish theory it is described as the Jaharkah scale transposed to Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat tonic). This means it is not merely ‘Ajam on another note’ in a loose classroom sense, but a maqam with a stable theoretical profile: a tonic on Ajam Ashiran, a strong center on Ajam, a leading tone at Husayni Ashiran, and a basically descending sayr.
resting_tones: Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat): the final tonic
Ajam (middle B-flat): the strong tone or qawi where an important secondary center appears
Husayni Ashiran (low E): the leading tone toward the final tonic in the Turkish description
motion_notes: Its sayr is descending in the classical theoretical description
It often begins or centers first in the upper or middle region before descending to confirm Ajam Ashiran as the final tonic
Its identity should not be reduced to the idea of a major scale on a lower B-flat, because sayr, emphasis, and the functions of the strong tone and leading tone are all part of its character
Its broad color is openly major/Jaharkah, but its maqamic identity remains more specific than a general major scale
common_path: Establish the Ajam/Jaharkah material in the upper or middle region
Confirm the strong tone on Ajam
Descend gradually through the maqam’s major framework
Close on Ajam Ashiran with a convincing cadence

common_modulations: It is close to Ajam in broad color, but differs mainly in the position of the tonic; Ajam Ushayran resolves on Ajam Ashiran rather than on the more common Arabic practical Ajam tonics
It is also very close to Jaharkah, since the classical Turkish description treats it as Jaharkah transposed to Ajam Ashiran; the difference is therefore primarily one of placement and tonal center
It differs from Rast because its third and broader tonal organization belong clearly to the Ajam/Jaharkah world rather than the Rast one
